questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings are, Purgatory, Delinquent and Surmise.

questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings are, Decanter, Osteoporosis and Sacrilegious.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which is the best substitute of the phrase. Present or found everywhere
questions.explanation: Omnipotent: Having unlimited power. Ephemeral: Lasting for a very short time. Ubiquitous: Present or found everywhere Evanescent: Synonym of ephemeral, lasting for a very short time.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which is the best substitute of the phrase. An arrangement to meet someone secretly
questions.explanation: Rendezvous: An arrangement to meet someone secretly at a time or place. Boulevard: a wide street in a town or city, typically one lined with trees Foyer: an entrance hall or other open area in a building used by the public, especially a hotel or theatre Lobby: a room providing a space out of which one or more other rooms or corridors lead, typically one near the entrance of a public building

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. When you're working for yourself, it's easy to be so caught up with your work that other major things like your health and wellbeing end up taking a back seat. Q. As important as it is to hustle, to be able to do your best work, you need to spend some time during the day not thinking about your work. R. Go for a jog, listen to music, read a book, meditate - there are so many useful ways in which you can spend your time to increase your productivity. S. And it's always a good idea to take a break every once in a while, to rejuvenate yourself.
questions.explanation: P begins by telling us the need for breaks in a hectic lifestyle. Q follows by giving us the importance of taking breaks. PQ is a pair: options (b) and (c) are eliminated. RS is pair (S is an extension of what all is being discussed in R). Option (d) is eliminated (Though R can also be the opening statement but the option (d) does not satisfy RQ pair clause). Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. But like they say, nothing worth having comes easy. Q. And that's the whole point of working for yourself, right? R. I actually spend longer hours working now than I did when I had a full-time job but there are so many wonderful things about being your own boss that all the hard work you have to put in is actually a lot of fun. S. Being your own boss is not easy.
questions.explanation: SP is a logical pair (S talks about being your own boss is not easy or relatively difficult and is followed by a popular saying that nothing worthwhile comes easy). RQ follow as a pair: R tells us of how this saying applies to the author specifically. Q concludes by referring to the wonderful things mentioned in R and telling us the point of it all. Option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given in either direct or indirect speech. Select the option that correctly converts the sentence in direct speech to indirect speech or vice versa. "My parents only have friends among former contract workers," said Larry.
options: Larry said that his parents only are having friends among former contract workers.
Larry said that his parents only had friends among former contract workers.
Larry said that his parents only have friends among former contract workers.
Larry said that his parents had only had friends among former contract workers.
questions.explanation: When converting direct to indirect speech, the simple present tense 'have' should change to the simple past 'had'. A is incorrect as 'are having' is the present continuous tense. C is incorrect as it does not change the tense. D is incorrect as 'had only had' is the past perfect tense. B is the right answer as 'had' is the simple past tense.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Stupor
questions.explanation: 'Stupor' means a state of near-unconsciousness or insensibility. This is equivalent to what 'daze' means, making option B, the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Fallacy
questions.explanation: 'Fallacy' means a mistaken belief, especially one based on unsound arguments. 'Delusion' refers to a belief or impression maintained despite being contradicted by reality or rational argument. Clearly, the two words are synonyms of each other, making option D, the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Parsimony
questions.explanation: 'Parsimony' means extreme unwillingness to spend money or use resources. 'Devious' means showing a skilful use of underhand tactics to achieve goals. 'Hasty' means done with excessive speed or urgency. 'Smorgasbord' means a wide range of something. Clearly, 'generosity' will be the correct antonym of parsimony. Option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Alleviate
questions.explanation: 'Alleviate' = 'mitigate' = to make (suffering, deficiency, or a problem) less severe. 'Stern' means strict. 'Aggravate' means to make something worse and will be the obvious synonym of alleviate. Option D will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the below question, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. I could not suppress the first violent emotion this action caused in my soul.
options: The first violent emotion caused by this action in my soul could not be suppressed by me.
The first violent emotion could not be suppressed by me that this action caused in my soul.
The first violent emotion could not be suppressed by me that was caused by this action in my soul.
The first violent emotion this action caused in my soul could not be suppressed by me.
questions.explanation: Option A is the right answer. Option C is eliminated as it makes it ambiguous as to what the sentence is actually attempting to convey. Options B and D are eliminated as they only convert a part of the original sentence into passive voice, and not the entire sentence.

questions.question: (i)
questions.explanation: Option b is the right answer, as it fits most appropriately in the context of the passage. Option A is eliminated as the word 'range' fits loosely in the passage but not as well as Option B. Option C is eliminated as the word 'count' does not fit in the passage. Option D is eliminated as the word 'level', does not bring out the meaning that the passage seeks to convey.

questions.question: (ii)
questions.explanation: Option d is the right answer, as it fits most appropriately in the context of the passage. Option A is eliminated as the word 'terrible' would be an overstatement in the context of the passage. Option C is eliminated as the word 'cumbersome' fits lsosely in the passage, but not as well as Option D. Option B is eliminated as the word 'troublemaking', which does not bring out the meaning that the passage seeks to convey.

questions.question: (iii)
questions.explanation: Option a is the right answer, as it fits most appropriately in the context of the passage. Option B is eliminated as the word 'foreground' makes no sense in the context. Option C is eliminated as the word 'backdrop' does not fit in the passage. Option D is eliminated as the word 'genetics', fits in the passage, but not as well as Option A.

questions.question: (iv)
questions.explanation: Option d is the right answer, as it fits most appropriately in the context of the passage. Option A is eliminated as the word 'prevent' means to stop something from happening, this makes no sense in the context. Option B is eliminated as the word 'incumbent' fits loosely in the passage, but not as well as Option D. Option C is eliminated as the word 'convalescent', which means a person recovering from an illness; this makes no sense in the context of the passage.

questions.question: (v)
questions.explanation: Option C is the right answer, as it fits most appropriately in the context of the passage. Option A is eliminated as the word 'pipeline' means a future plan, fits loosely in the passage, but not as well as Option C. Option B is eliminated as the word 'itinerary' does not fit in the passage. Option D is eliminated as the word 'list', does not bring out the meaning that the passage seeks to convey.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase given below. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. got the wrong end of the stick
questions.explanation: To get the wrong end of the stick means to take or understand something in a wrong way. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase highlighted in the sentence. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. without ceremony
questions.explanation: Ceremony refers to the formal activities conducted on some solemn or important occasion. Synonyms: formal, official etc. Therefore, without ceremony means to be informal, unofficial, intimate etc. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions, a part of the sentence is in BOLD that may contain an error. Four alternatives are given for the bracketed bold portion. Identify the one which replaces the underlined part to form a logically and grammatically correct statement. Rashida would not have failed if (she had good money).
options: she would have had good money
questions.explanation: This is a typical case of conditionals. The second clause should be in past perfect tense.Also, 'enough' is a better choice to depict the context than 'good' here. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A part of the sentence is underlined. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed bold part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose "No Improvement" option. One of the articles (which was published) in the magazine recently was written by me
questions.explanation: Here 'which' is used as a relative pronoun of 'articles'. Since 'articles' is plural 'which' should also take a plural verb. The sentence means that among the articles published in the magazine one was written by me. The second 'was' in the sentence refers to the one written by me.

questions.question: Some parts of the given sentence have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" option. His family life is a failure (1)/ he is always at (2)/ loggers-head with his wife.(3)/ No Error(4)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is "His family life is a failure; he is always at logger-heads with his wife". Loggers-head is a wrong usage. The phrase 'at loggerheads' means to be in disagreement.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error and choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" option. Let us do more (1)/ because today I am (2)/ in good spirit.(3)/ No Error(4)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is "Let us do more because today I am in high spirits". Spirit means ghost or soul, but spirits mean alcohol or mood. Here the subject is referring to his mood. So 'good spirit' should be replaced by 'high spirits'.

questions.question: Four alternatives A, B, C and D are given under each sentence, you are required to select the most suitable alternative to fill in the blank in the sentence to make it meaningful. The president called upon politicians not to______ themselves with communal and parochial forces.
questions.explanation: 'Align themselves' means to support. Now the sentence has the meaning 'The president does not want the politicians to support communal and parochial forces'. None of the other options make the given sentence coherent.

questions.question: Four alternatives A, B, C and D are given under each sentence, you are required to select the most suitable alternative to fill in the blank in the sentence to make it meaningful. If arms race goes on between the super powers, chances for world peace will be.........
questions.explanation: Jeopardize means to put something in danger, which fits here. Linger - wait around, stay. Nip - bite or pinch sharply. D is the right answer.
